content: Hey, I'm thinking about going to visit my sister in Portland next month. She just had her second kid and I haven't met the baby yet.
That's exciting! Portland is beautiful this time of year. Do you have specific dates in mind for the trip?
Probably the first week of June. I need to check my work schedule at MIT. Robotics research has been intense lately with the new project deadline.
MIT must keep you busy. What's the new robotics project about?

It's an autonomous underwater drone for coral reef monitoring. Really fascinating work, but it means long hours in the lab. Speaking of work, I'm also trying to figure out what to do with Ziggy while I'm away.
Ah, your border collie! Have you found a pet sitter in Raleigh yet?
Not yet. I just moved here from Boston six months ago and haven't built up a network. My neighbor might watch him, but I'm not sure. Ziggy gets anxious when I'm gone too long.
That's tough with a new city. Maybe there's a local dog-walking service that does pet sitting? What does Ziggy like to do for fun?

He loves the dog park near our apartment and going on hikes. We've been exploring the trails around Umstead State Park. I've also been getting into mountain biking myself, so we're both outdoorsy types.
Sounds like you two are a perfect match for outdoor adventures! Have you tried any of the mountain biking trails near Raleigh yet?

Yeah, I've done a few at Lake Crabtree. Still getting used to the humidity compared to Boston though. Hey, do you know any good Japanese restaurants in Portland? I've been missing decent ramen since moving.

Portland has amazing Japanese food! I'd recommend trying Afuri for ramen - they have a yuzu shio that's incredible. Since you're into photography too, you might want to check out the Japanese Garden while you're there.
Perfect, I'll add that to my list. I've been getting more into photography lately, mostly landscape and pet photography of Ziggy. Maybe I'll finally get some good shots of the Pacific Northwest.
The PNW is incredibly photogenic! June should give you great lighting. Are you planning to visit any other spots while you're there?

Probably just stick to Portland and maybe a day trip to the coast. I want to spend most of the time with family. Oh, and I need to remember to bring some of that home brew beer I made last month as a gift.
Home brewing too? You're quite the Renaissance person! What kind of beer did you make?
An IPA with some experimental hops I found at a local shop here. I think my brother-in-law will appreciate it. Alright, I should probably start planning this trip for real. Thanks for the chat!
Anytime! Have a wonderful visit with your sister and the new baby. Don't forget to get some family photos with your camera!
